findings.problem: Sanitization drift in the close: the chapter resolves into reassuring thematic summary ("women got this child born", "choose each other before the world approved") instead of staying inside immediate legal/medical jeopardy. This functions as a moral safety valve that defuses pressure at the exact consequence point.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the ending block at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:313-325 to keep scene-level danger embodied. Replace abstract solidarity phrasing with concrete unresolved stakes in-room (postpartum risk checks, flood contamination, legal exposure once Tom or authorities appear). Keep the kinship choice, but dramatize it through action and reaction rather than thesis statements.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 313-325 contain at least two concrete unresolved hazards (for example: a specific postpartum complication risk plus a specific legal/custody risk) rendered as active scene beats, and the final paragraph ends on a concrete consequence/action. FAIL if the close relies on abstract affirmational summary language as the primary emotional landing.

findings.problem: Timidity in postpartum embodiment: "tender stitches and afterpain" remains euphemistic and non-specific in a scene that otherwise commits to physical labor detail. The prose retreats from concrete bodily reality right after delivery.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:317-325, replace euphemistic placeholders with specific, character-true postpartum sensations and care actions (movement constraints, bleeding management, stitch pain behavior, cleanup logistics under flood conditions).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 317-325 include at least one precise postpartum bodily detail and one linked practical care action visible on-page. FAIL if postpartum state is described mainly via broad labels (for example, "afterpain") without concrete manifestation.

findings.problem: The guardian declaration lands with insufficient dramatic resistance. The chapter states future dispute risk but gives Elsie little immediate opposition cost, so the climax reads administratively resolved rather than consequentially contested.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the decision sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:285-315 by adding at least one immediate obstacle (procedural, social, or reputational) that forces Elsie to reaffirm the instruction under pressure. Keep Keane/Bhatti/Arthur present, but make support conditional or risky instead of frictionless.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 285-315 include at least one explicit counter-pressure beat against the declaration and Elsie makes a specific on-page choice to proceed despite that cost. FAIL if all surrounding characters remain uniformly supportive without meaningful resistance.

findings.problem: Contraction/register drift for Noreen (style bible: high contraction level). Under high scene pressure she uses uncontracted forms ("You are not alone", "You are sure?") without a clear emphasis marker, producing stiffness against the chapter's spoken rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Noreen's lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:175 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:297 toward her high-contraction, pressure-responsive register (colloquial, clipped, intimate). Retain any uncontracted form only if the line explicitly signals deliberate emphasis.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised spans at lines 175 and 297, Noreen's default delivery uses contractions and sounds distinct from Keane's clinical diction; any remaining full-form line must carry explicit emphasis in adjacent text. FAIL if uncontracted phrasing remains neutral/default.

findings.problem: Supporting voices compress toward the same clipped-instruction cadence (Arthur, Keane, Mrs Bhatti), reducing idiolect separation in key logistics beats.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework dialogue at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:49-66 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:245-314 so each speaker carries role-specific lexical texture: Arthur in infrastructure/task language, Keane in procedural clinical sequencing, Bhatti in shop/community-credit pragmatics.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised target spans contain at least one line per speaker (Arthur, Keane, Bhatti) that is attributable without tags because syntax/lexicon is distinct. FAIL if lines remain largely interchangeable when dialogue tags are removed.

findings.problem: Recurring comparison scaffold ("as if") appears four times at emotional hinges, creating habitual interpretive distance instead of direct sensory immediacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:205, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:253 by replacing at least two "as if" clauses with direct physical perception or action beats.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter uses no more than two "as if" constructions total, and the revised target lines read as immediate sensory/behavioral observation rather than inferred analogy. FAIL if all four current "as if" hinges remain.

findings.problem: Repeated reaction template ("looked at" beat at turn points) appears more than twice and flattens dramatic modulation.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:319, vary reaction beats toward touch/sound/spatial-pressure responses aligned with the style profile's sensory bias.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the listed lines are rewritten without a gaze-verb reaction beat and still preserve turn clarity. FAIL if three or more emotional pivots in those spans still rely on "looked at" construction.

summary: Chapter 17 delivers strong causal logistics and a clear state delta (safe delivery plus guardian declaration), but it does not clear pass-level quality under Booker/craft standards: the close sanitizes pressure into thematic reassurance, some postpartum embodiment retreats into euphemism, dialogue register drifts toward stiffness/interchangeability in places, and recurring prose templates (notably "as if" and gaze beats) flatten line-level texture.
